title: Tables Generated by Compilation

Tables Generated by Compilation

Tables Generated by Compilation

During component compilation, the compiler creates internal tables of compilation information. You can choose to add information from these tables to compilation messages.

For more information, see /inf.

Table 1. Compilation Tables
Table Description
Model Definitions Table Contains an entry for each component entity and each field in the component entity, regardless of whether it is included in the component structure.

The maximum number of entries in this table is limited to 1023. Each entry in the table takes up 64 bytes. The model definitions table is one of the largest of the internal tables created during component compilation.
Record interface Table Contains an entity descriptor for each entity in the component. The table is used to create DBMS driver control blocks and also governs the sequence of fields in the entity. The greater the number of fields and entities in a component, the larger this table gets. However, it rarely makes up a significant proportion of the total table space, as other tables such as the paint and entity tables are usually much larger than this table.
Paint Reference Table Information about the painted fields, that is, fields included in the component structure and layout.
Paint Information Table Foreground information and background information about the component. The size of the component governs the size of this table.

The size of a component can be calculated as the character position of the end of the right-most item on the component multiplied by the line number of the lowest item on the component. For example, a component that is painted 132 characters wide by 60 characters deep contains 132 times 60, or 7920 character cells.

Each character cell requires a total of four bytes of storage in the paint information table (two bytes each for the foreground and background information).
Paint Labels Table Label frames defined for the component.
Component Variables Table Component variables defined for the component. Each component variable uses 24 bytes.
Key Table Information about the keys used in the entities. There is one entry per key field defined for the component. Each entry in the key table uses 24 bytes. If a component contains three entities, and there are two keys per entity, the key table is 6 times 24 bytes, or 144 bytes.
ProcScript Vector Table The ProcScript vector table contains an entry for every trigger in the component containing ProcScript. This includes triggers containing only Uniface default ProcScript.
Input Translation Table Contains a copy of the compiled translation on input table.
Output Translation Table Contains a copy of the compiled translation on output table.
Literal Label Pool Contains any literals used within the component, such as component names, field names, entity names and models. There is no ProcScript literal information in this table.
Literal Misc Pool Contains information on any initial field values and the component title.
Structure Table Reflects the component structure. It holds all the selected information for the component and the necessary status information. For more information, see the following section.

Structure Table

The structure table reflects the painted structure in the component. This is a prototype for the run-time system. This table holds all the selected information for the component. All the necessary status information is also stored in this table. This information is divided into the following categories:

  • The status of the user input, for example, the data to be checked, the character attribute such as bold or underline, or the current text position.
  • The status of the DBMS input, for example, the current segment of the hitlist plus any caching information.

The structure table information is a subset of the total transaction set built during a retrieve or during data entry. For example, if a component has an entity painted with six repeated occurrences, the occurrences displayed when the user activates ^RETRIEVE will be stored in this table. If the user positions the cursor on the sixth occurrence and presses next occurrence, the first occurrence is dropped from the structure table, the next five occurrences are moved up, and the newly retrieved occurrence is buffered in the structure table.

For 16-bit (and smaller) versions of Uniface only, the structure table is limited to 64 kilobytes. If this limit is exceeded, the component compiler displays an error message.

To reduce the size of the structure table, you can try the following:

  • Reduce the occurrence repetition of the entities. The more entities and fields that are painted inside a repeated entity, the greater the impact of this reduction.
  • Comment out unused triggers if they refer to fields which are not painted or used elsewhere in the triggers. The effect of this action depends on how deep the fields are painted in the component; the deeper they are painted, the greater the impact.
  • Change the structure of the component.
Was this topic helpful?
